published_title: Do Health Insurers Matter for Health Outcomes? Evidence from a Large Natural Experiment in Colombia
authors: Giancarlo Buitrago; Paul Rodriguez-Lesmes; Natalia Serna; Marcos Vera-Hernández
note: Retitled from the IFS/CEPR working paper 'The Role of Hospital Networks in Individual Mortality' (IFS WP202421, 2024). Natalia Serna's research page lists it as 'Conditionally Accepted, Review of Economics and Statistics' (same 4 authors, same Colombia insurer-termination natural experiment). Counted as forthcoming (conditionally accepted). Caveat: an earlier web-search snapshot of her CV described it as 'R&R at ReStat'; the research page is more current, so forthcoming is the most-advanced verified status.

Author Giacomo Mantegazza's website lists the paper as 'Conditionally Accepted' at the American Economic Review. An extended abstract appeared in EC'23 proceedings. Also circulated as SSRN 4032999 / arXiv 2202.05946. Conditionally accepted -> forthcoming.

Jihye Jeon's research page shows the project has split into two papers, both invited revisions (R&R): 'Investment and Usage of the Subsea Internet Cable Network' (Oct 2025) 'Under revision, Journal of Political Economy' (matches the conference subtitle, listed as primary here), and 'International Trade in Data on the Subsea Internet Cable Network' (Jul 2025) 'Under revision, American Economic Journal: Microeconomics.' Both are R&R at named journals.

published_title: Diversification, Market Entry, and the Global Internet Backbone
authors: El Hadi Caoui; Andrew L. Steck
note: Retitled from 'Network Diversity, Market Entry, and the Global Internet Backbone'. Accepted and published online as a Review of Economic Studies advance article (DOI 10.1093/restud/rdaf097, 2025); not yet assigned to a print issue, so treated as forthcoming/in-press. Restud.com also lists it. Verified on Oxford Academic publisher page.

authors: Milena Almagro; Felipe Barbieri; Juan Camilo Castillo; Nathaniel Hickok; Tobias Salz
note: Both Juan Camilo Castillo's research page ('Revise and resubmit, Econometrica. Last updated: October 2025') and Felipe Barbieri's CV (Oct 2025, '(R&R, Econometrica)') confirm R&R at Econometrica. NBER WP 32185; MIT posts a 'manuscript_resubmission' file.

authors: Benjamin Klopack; Eric Lewis; Fernando Luco
note: Listed under 'Working Papers' on both Ben Klopack's research page and Fernando Luco's site; no journal/R&R indicated. SSRN 5052831 / Kilts Center working paper. No acceptance or invited revision found on author CVs.

authors: Adam Dearing; Lorenzo Magnolfi; Daniel Quint; Christopher J. Sullivan; Sarah B. Waldfogel
note: NBER WP 32863 (2024) / SSRN 4936899. Listed under 'Working Papers' on both Lorenzo Magnolfi's site and Christopher Sullivan's site, with no journal, R&R, or acceptance noted.

authors: Daniel Deisenroth; Utsav Manjeer; Zarak Sohail; Steven Tadelis; Nils Wernerfelt
note: NBER WP 32726 (2024) / SSRN 4887993. Listed under 'Working Papers' (2024) on Nils Wernerfelt's research page with no publication status; no R&R or acceptance found.

authors: Maria Garcia-Osipenko; Nicholas Vreugdenhil; Nahim Bin Zahur
note: Nahim Zahur's research page: 'with Maria Garcia-Osipenko and Nicholas Vreugdenhil, 2025. R&R at Journal of Political Economy.' (Agenda's 'Maria Osipenko' = Maria Garcia-Osipenko.) Also SSRN 5217587 / Queen's QED WP 1536.

authors: Ingrid Huitfeldt; Victoria Marone; Daniel C. Waldinger
note: Ingrid Huitfeldt's publications page: 'Huitfeldt, I., Marone, V., Waldinger, D. (2026). ... Accepted at the American Economic Review.' Accepted -> forthcoming (not yet assigned to an issue). Originally NBER WP 32458 (2024).

authors: Ashvin Gandhi; Brett Hollenbeck; Zhijian Li
note: Brett Hollenbeck's CV (Feb 2026), Working Papers section: 'Misinformation and Mistrust... (with Ashvin Gandhi and Li Zhijian) [Revise and Resubmit at American Economic Review].' NBER WP 34161 (2025). (Note: an earlier Gandhi faculty-page snapshot mentioned J. of Finance; the Feb-2026 CV says AER.)

published_title: Randomized Entry: The Equilibrium Effects of Entry in Digital Financial Markets
note: Francis Annan's research page marks the paper '[REVISE & RESUBMIT, AMERICAN ECONOMIC REVIEW]'. Full/expanded title now 'Randomized Entry: The Equilibrium Effects of Entry in Digital Financial Markets'. NBER WP 33134 (2024) / SSRN 5016367. Single author.
Felipe Barbieri's CV (Oct 2025) lists it under 'Working Papers' as his Job Market Paper (with Gregory Dobbels; Paul Taubman Prize 2025). No journal, R&R, or acceptance indicated.
Garima Sharma's website lists the paper as 'Revise and Resubmit, American Economic Review.' Working paper dated Nov 2025. Single author.
